repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: New York State Council on the Arts Electronic Media and Film Program records

origination.label: creator
origination.persname: New York State Council on the Arts., Electronic Media and Film Program.

did.abstract: Records from the Electronic Media and Film Program (EMF) and the Individual Artists Program (IND) include media field reports and surveys, files on organizations and individual artists, support for production, distributions, and organization, special projects files, film festival catalogs, Set in Motion records, books, reports, and other publications. Also includes media materials such as video, film, tape recordings, photographs, as well as digital files.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Electronic Media and Film (EMF) Program is dedicated to furthering artistic growth and public engagement in the media arts. EMF supports a variety of activities enabling diverse constituents to participate in the development, exhibition and appreciation of film and electronic media, including moving image, sound, and technology as an art form. To ensure diversity within the media arts, EMF welcomes and supports a range of non-profit organizations including technology centers, independent cinemas, museums, galleries and broadcasters as well as internet-based dissemination. EMF has a long-standing commitment to creative exploration and exhibition in all aspects of the media arts, including film and media appreciation, professional development, technology and new modes of exhibition, distribution and interdisciplinary work.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Records from the Electronic Media and Film Program (EMF) and the Individual Artists Program (IND) include media field reports and surveys, files on organizations and individual artists, support for production, distributions, and organization, special projects files, film festival catalogs, Set in Motion records, books, reports, and other publications. Also includes media materials such as video, film, tape recordings, photographs, as well as digital files.

odd.head: NOTES
odd.p: Forms part of the Rose Goldsen Archive of New Media Art.
